This is held in a Mandatory Provident Fund (MPF) account at AIA.  MPF is a self funded retirement account which is required by Hong Kong law to be established and funded out of a person's income while they are resident in Hong Kong.

Sullivan & Cromwell LLP Group Defined Benefit Plan for Partners
I continue to participate in this plan but no new contributions will be made on behalf after my separation from the firm. (2004-01-01)
I will receive my percentage of all partnership profits earned up until I separate from the partnership.  I will receive my final payment before I assume my duties of my position. (2021-11-01)
Capital will be paid back to me shortly after my separation (2021-11-01)
Sullivan & Cromwell LLP Supplemental Pension Plan for Partners

I will be cashed out of this plan and paid the cash value shortly after separation from the firm. I will no longer participate in the plan after separation and no further contributions will be made on my behalf. (2004-01-01)

Pursuant to Hong Kong’s Mandatory Provident Fund Schemes Ordinance (Cap. 485), my former employer Sullivan & Cromwell was required to contribute to a retirement account established on my behalf. I continue to participate in this defined contribution plan. Sullivan & Cromwell stopped making contributions after I ceased to be resident in Hong Kong in January 2020. (2011-01-01)
